title: Two Seas That Meet but Never Mix — The Qur'an Described an Invisible Wall
_note: CINEMATIC, hook-first. Footage = ocean/water (no humans; thumbnail-verified). NO recital. Simple English. SOURCE: Surah Ar-Rahman 55:19-20 (and Al-Furqan 25:53) — 'He released the two seas meeting; between them a barrier they do not transgress.' SCIENCE (solid): a halocline/pycnocline — a sharp salinity/temperature density gradient — acts as a barrier between water masses (e.g., Atlantic vs Mediterranean at the Strait of Gibraltar, which keep distinct temperature/salinity). ACCURACY GUARD: framed on the REAL invisible density barrier, NOT the misleading viral 'two-color oceans' photo (that's temporary sediment). Safe-zone captions. #HistoryTikTokContest.

segments.text: There are places in the ocean where two seas meet, and refuse to mix. The Quran described this fourteen hundred years ago.
It said: He released the two seas, meeting together. But between them is a barrier they do not cross.
For centuries this sounded impossible. Water is water. How can two seas not mix?
Then oceanographers found it. Where seas of different temperature and saltiness meet, an invisible wall forms between them.
Each sea keeps its own temperature, its own saltiness, its own life. Held apart by a barrier made of nothing but density.
You cannot see it. But it is there. Exactly where the Quran said it would be.
Between the two seas is a barrier, which they do not cross.
A wall you cannot see, named in a book thirteen centuries before we found it.

sources: Qur'an, Surah Ar-Rahman 55:19-20 (and Al-Furqan 25:53) — 'He released the two seas, meeting [side by side]; between them is a barrier [which] they do not transgress.'

Oceanography: a halocline/pycnocline is a sharp density gradient (salinity + temperature) that resists mixing between water masses; e.g. Atlantic and Mediterranean water at the Strait of Gibraltar keep distinct temperature/salinity for hundreds of km (Britannica; Wikipedia: Halocline/Pycnocline).
ACCURACY: framed on the real invisible density barrier, NOT the misleading viral 'two oceans don't mix' two-colour photo (that is temporary sediment, and overclaimed).
Qur'an 55:20 shown as Arabic + English; not recited.
VISUALS: all Pexels clips verified by thumbnail — no human faces/bodies.
